events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: field service, HVAC-R, brazing & evacuation. Observed: vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read gauge, FD). Work rough-complete. References: mfr specs, ACCA, EPA 608. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since vacuum decay test failed (not held at target microns), evacuation non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk? If refrigerant system not properly evacuated, moisture/acids cause compressor damage, maybe high. Not critical immediate safety? But non-compliant and can damage asset. Risk high maybe. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value numeric reading? Not provided; could use 0 or null? Schema says value: 0 default. Since no numeric reading given, keep 0. Findings: vacuum decay test failed, system not evacuated to manufacturer target, potential moisture/contaminants. Actions: leak check, repair leaks, re-evacuate to target microns, verify decay rate holds, replace filter drier if needed, document. Rationale: evacuation must achieve and hold target vacuum per mfr/ACCA; failure indicates leaks/outgassing/contamination. References: mfr specs, ACCA, EPA 608 (recovery/venting). Need ensure JSON valid.

{
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed: system did not hold target microns.",
    "Evacuation is incomplete/non-compliant, indicating possible leaks, outgassing, or contamination.",
    "Moisture and non-condensables may remain in the refrigerant circuit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform a thorough leak check on the entire refrigerant circuit and repair any leaks.",
    "Replace the liquid-line filter drier if opened to atmosphere or contaminated.",
    "Re-evacuate the system with a deep vacuum pump to manufacturer-specified target microns.",
    "Repeat the vacuum decay test (micron rise test) and confirm it holds at target.",
    "Document final vacuum readings and decay rate before charging."
  ],
  "rationale": "A proper evacuation must reach and hold the OEM/ACCA target vacuum level; a failed decay test means the system is not ready for refrigerant charge and violates manufacturer and industry requirements.",
  "references": [
    "mfr specs",
    "ACCA",
    "EPA 608"
  ]
}
